features.summary: UnitedHealth Group reported Q4 2024 revenues exceeding $400 billion and adjusted EPS of $27.66, within guidance ranges despite significant challenges. The medical care ratio was 150 basis points above original outlook due to member mix changes, Medicaid rate lag, cyberattack costs, and accelerated specialty drug prescribing. The company navigated approximately $6 billion in unanticipated impacts while maintaining growth across all segments. For 2025, UHG expects to serve 1.9 million additional people with revenues approaching $340 billion for UnitedHealthcare and strong growth across Optum divisions.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed confidence in 2025 pricing adequacy and expects full-year medical care ratio of 86.5% ±50 basis points, 100 basis points above 2024 levels. The company maintains its long-term 13-16% growth objective and expects cash flow from operations to approach $33 billion. UHG anticipates Medicare Advantage growth of up to 800,000 members, Optum Health serving 5.4 million value-based care patients, and continued strong retention rates. Management emphasized ongoing investments in digital capabilities and AI-driven efficiencies to improve consumer experience.
features.risks: Key risks include continued Medicare rate cuts in the second year of the three-year CMS reduction cycle, ongoing hospital coding intensity increases, and accelerated specialty drug prescribing trends. The company faces potential PBM reform legislation and regulatory scrutiny. Medicaid rate updates continue to lag member health status changes, though this gap is expected to narrow. The elevated care activity environment from 2024 is assumed to persist into 2025, requiring careful medical management.
features.key_drivers: Growth is driven by strong Medicare Advantage retention and returning members, balanced commercial and public sector expansion, and continued Optum segment momentum. Digital engagement increased significantly with mobile app visits up 66% year-over-year and overall digital engagement up 33%. The company is implementing 100% PBM rebate pass-through by 2028 and investing heavily in value-based care expansion. Operating cost improvements of 150 basis points in 2024 are expected to continue, driven by AI implementation and digital transformation initiatives.
